Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 192 (+) [Udug-hul Compendium][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
x465single ruling after each line, except for the colophon
Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] ⸢e₃?⸣ uŋ₃ kalam-ma an-ta ki-ta lu₃-lu₃

(o 1) [...] ermerged (Akkadian: and) disturbed the people of the land, above (Akkadian: and) below.

[... u₂]-⸢ṣa?⸣-ma ni-iš KUR e-liš u šap-liš id-luḫ
o 2o 2

[...] ⸢RA? la-ba-ŋar u₄⸣-šu₂-uš-ta ŋar-⸢ŋar⸣-ra

(o 2) [...] will not appease [...] ... (Akkadian: the land), (but) will display gloom;

AN [...] a-na ma-a-ti la i-nu-uḫ-ḫu ⸢da⸣-um-ma-tu₄ i-šak-ka-nu
o 3o 3

[...] ⸢gal⸣-gal-la nam-lu-u₁₉-lu sila-daŋal-la al-bu-bu-de₃-ne

(o 3) [...] (Akkadian: the šēdu-demon, the utukku-demon, the rābiṣu-demon), the powerful ones, who keep chasing around people in the open spaces;

še-⸢e⸣-[du u₂]-⸢tuk⸣-ku ra-bi-⸢ṣu?⸣ GAL-MEŠ ša₂ ni-ši ina re-ba-a-te it-ta-na-aš-ra-bi-ṭu
o 4o 4

ud ḫuš ud gal an-eden-na gaba-bi nu-gi₄-gi₄ a-ga-bi-še₃ <igi> nu-un-bar-ra

(o 4) the furious storm (Akkadian: the uhušgallu-storm), the great storm which does not turn back in the open country, which does not look behind itself;

u₄-ḫuš-gal-lu-u u₄-mu ra-bu-u ša₂ ina ṣe-ri₃ i-rat-⸢su⸣ la ut-tar-ru ar₂-ki-šu₂ ⸢la ip⸣-pal-la-su
o 5o 5

du-gur nam-uru₁₆-na e-sir₂ uŋ₃ degₓ(RI)-degₓ(RI)-ga tur maḫ-gin₇ a-ga-bi-še₃ nu-tak₄-tak₄

(o 5) the great Ugur who gathers people up (Akkadian: Erra who makes people fall) in the street, who does not leave the small one (or) the great one behind him;

dER₃.RA ra-ba-a ša₂ ina ⸢su⸣-qi₂ ni-ši u₂-šam-qa-tu₂ ṣe-eḫ-⸢ra?⸣ ra-ba-a ana ar-ki-šu₂ la iz-zi-bu
o 6o 6

dmaškim—GI₆—lu₂—ḫar-ra-an e₂-a mu-un-ḫu-luḫ-e-de₃ e₂-⸢gi₄⸣-a tur-ra e₂-šag₄-ga nu-tak₄-tak₄

(o 6) the Maškim-gi-lu-harran-demon (Akkadian: the hallulūʾa-demon) who terrifies (Akkadian: in) the household, who does not leave the bride behind, the young girl in her1 bedroom;

ḫal-lu-lu-u₂-a ša₂ ina E₂ u₂-gal-la-⸢tu⸣ kal-la-tu₂ ṣe!(AD-)eḫ-ḫer-ti ina ur-šu₂-šu₂ la u₂-sa-⸢at?-tu₂⸣
o 7o 7

aš-gar aš-ru e₂ gig-ta gi₄-gi₄-⸢ba⸣ uŋ₃ kur daŋal-la dab-dab-be₂

(o 7) the ašbur-disease (Akkadian: the diʾû-disease), the shivering that bitterly strikes the household, (that) binds people in the vast land;

di-iʾ-u ⸢šur⸣-pu-u ša₂ E₂ mar-ṣi-iš i-šab-bi-ṭu UN-MEŠ KUR DAGALti₃ i-kam-mu-u
o 8o 8

[...] x x TA UD NA₄ AMA ⸢ib₂⸣ gig ḫaš₂ gig GU-⸢ŠE?⸣-gin₇ ⸢e₂?⸣ šag₄-ga bar-ra niŋin-e

(o 8) [...] ... the hip illness, the thigh illness encircle the household inside (and) outside like a ...

[...] x x x x [...] x x x x x x x [...]
o 99

[...] x [...]

(o 9) [...] ... [...]

o 10o 10

[x x] x šub-ba bad₃ ⸢gul?⸣ [...]

(o 10) [...] will make ... fall; will destroy the city wall [...]

[x x] ⸢TI?⸣ u₂-šam-qa-tu₂ ⸢du-ra⸣ [...]
o 11o 11

[...] aš!(DIŠ-)gar sag₃-ge-de₃ gag-šub-ba ⸢šag₄⸣ [gig ...]

(o 11) [...] the ašŋar-illness will strike, the gagšubba-illness, the heart illness (Akkadian: the one who hastens (his) body) [...]

[... mu-ḫa]-am-miṭ ⸢zu⸣-ru x [...]
o 12o 12

[ddim₉]-me ddim₉-me-a [...] ⸢MI? ŋar?⸣-[ra? ...]

(o 12) Dimme (and) Dimmea (Akkadian: Lamaštu and the labāṣu-demon that [...] in the city and) [...] will afflict [...]

MIN<(dDIM₉.ME)> dDIM₉.ME.A ša₂ ina IRI ⸢u⸣ [...]
o 13o 13

⸢uŋ₃⸣ iri im-ma-an-šeŋ₆-[...]

(o 13) they will give burning pain to the city's people (Akkadian: the inhabited world) [...]

⸢ni⸣-[ši] ⸢da-ad⸣-mi u₂-ṣar-⸢ra⸣-[pu ...]
o 14o 14

ur-⸢saŋ⸣ [den-ki]-ke₄ ur-gin₇ E ⸢NE?⸣ [...]

(o 14) the hero Enki (Akkadian: Ea) [...] thus [...]

qar-⸢ra⸣-[du d]e₂-a ki-a-⸢a⸣-[am ...]1
o 15o 15

nam-lu₂-[ulu₃] ⸢a₂⸣ nir-ra-bi [...]

(o 15) the people whose pure2 strength [...]

ni-ši ⸢ša₂ e⸣-mu-qa!(KAD₃-)⸢su?⸣ [...]2
o 1616

lu₂-mu₇-[mu₇ ...]3

(o 16) the incantation priest [...]

(rest of obverse missing)
Reverse
r 1r 1

kuš-gu₄-[gal-la ...]

(r 1) The kušgugal-drum [...] (Akkadian: ditto (the kušgugalu-drum) which [...] in the body of [...])

MIN<(KUŠ.GU₄.GAL)> ša₂ ina zu-⸢mur⸣ [...]
r 2r 2

kuš-gu₄-gal-[la ...]

(r 2) the kušgugal-drum [...] (Akkadian: ditto (the kušgugalu-drum) in [...])

MIN<(KUŠ.GU₄.GAL)>u₂ ina [...]
r 3r 3

kuš-gu₄-gal-⸢la⸣ [...]

(r 3) the kušgugal-drum [...] (Akkadian: ditto (the kušgugalu-drum) [...] may it chase the foe from [...]!

MIN<(KUŠ.GU₄.GAL)>u₂ ina [...] ⸢lem-nu⸣ [li]-⸢ir⸣-[du-ud]
r 4r 4

⸢ḫul-ŋal₂⸣ [x x] ⸢kar₂!(ŠA₃-)ra erim₂-ma ḫe₂-en-da-[ab-sar-ra]

(r 4) May it (the drum) expel the evil [...] the slanderer, the enemy!

[...] a-a-⸢ba⸣ liṭ-ru-[ud]
r 5r 5

[... ḫe₂-en]-⸢zi⸣-ir-zi-ir niŋ₂-sag₁₀-⸢ga⸣ ḫe₂-en-ŋa₂-[ŋa₂]

(r 5) [...] (Akkadian: May it erase [...])! May it display goodness!

[... li-pa]-⸢as-sis⸣ dum-qa ⸢liš⸣-tak-[kin]
r 6r 6

[...] x x x ⸢daŋal⸣-la šag₄-bi ḫe₂-en-[...]

(r 6) [...] wide ..., may his heart (Akkadian: his mind) [...]

[...] x x ⸢MI⸣ ŠU x DU MA ka-bat-⸢ta-šu liš⸣-[...]
r 7r 7

[...] ⸢ḫul-ŋal₂⸣-e ḫul-⸢luḫ⸣-ḫa [...]

(r 7) [...] the evil [...] (Akkadian: to) [...] fear [...]

[...] ⸢lem⸣-nu a-na [...]
r 8r 8

[... mu]-un-gal-e e₂-a mu-un-[...]

(r 8) [...] is big, in the temple ... [...]

[...] ra-bu-u ina E₂ ⸢ZU⸣ x [...]
r 9r 9

[e₂] ⸢u₆⸣-nir mu-un-gal-e eridugki-ga [...]

(r 9) (Akkadian: [...]) the temple of the ziggurat is big; in Eridu [...]

[...] ⸢E₂!(KID) MIN<(U₆.NIR)> ra-bu-u ina eri-du₁₀ [...]
r 10r 10

[e₂] ⸢u₆⸣-nir ḫi-li ⸢du₈?-a?⸣-ni eridugki-[ga ...]

(r 10) The temple of the ziggurat is endowed with attractiveness; (in) Eridu [...]

[...]
r 11r 11

[...] x [... eridug]⸢ki-ga⸣-ke₄ [...]

(r 11) [...] ... (Akkadian: the sage, the exorcist) of Eridu [...]

⸢ap-kal⸣-lu a-ši-pu ša₂ ⸢eri-du₁₀⸣
r 12r 12

tu₆ nam-ti-la en gal den-ki-⸢ga⸣-ke₄

(r 12) Incantation of life, (incantation) of the great lord, Enki (Akkadian: Ea)

ši-pat ba-la-ṭi ša₂ EN GALe de₂-a
r 1313

udug!(GIDIM) ḫul a-la₂ ḫul bar-še₃ ḫe₂-em-ta-⸢gub-ba⸣

(r 13) May the evil udug-demon, the evil ala-demon stand outside!

r 1414

udug!(GIDIM) sag₁₀-ga dlamma sag₁₀-ga ḫe₂-en-<da>-sug₂-sug₂-de-eš

(r 14) May the favourable udug-deity, the favourable lammar-deity firmly stand (by him)!

r 1515

ka-inim-ma kuš-gu₄-gal-la-ke₄4

(r 15) Incantation formula of the kušgugal-drum.

(catchline)
r 1616

EGIR-šu₂ en₂ urudu<niŋ₂>-kalag-ga ḫur-saŋ-ta du₃-a

(r 16) After this: incantation: "With the drum made in the mountain".

(colophon)
r 1717

GIM LIBIR-šu₂ GIŠ-ma ba₃-ri₂ GIŠ mqur-di—LUGAL.IR₉.RA lu₂ŠAB.TUR a-ga-aš-gu-u

(r 17) According to its original, written and checked. Written document of Qurdi-Nergal, the scribal apprentice, the novice.

r 1818

ša IR d60 lit-bal-šu ša₂ ina šur-qu i-ša₂-riq ša₂ ina dan-na-an e-kim dNA₃ ZAḪ₂ liq-bi

(r 18) Whoever takes (this tablet) away, may Ea take him away! Whoever steals it by theft or removes (it) by force, may Nabu decree (his) annihilation!

r 1919

[ina LA₂]ṣi md30—PAP—TU MAN KUR aš-šur itiAB U₄ 8-KAM₂ lim-me mḫa-na-an lu₂ša₂-kin₃ iritil—bar-si-bi

(r 19) In the time of Sennacherib, king of the land of Aššur. Ṭebetu (Month X), 8th day; eponym: Hananu, governor of Til-Barsip (701 BC).

r 2020

NIR.GAL₂-ZU NA.AN.TEŠ₂ dTU.TU

(r 20) Nabu, may he who trusts in you not be ashamed!

1First signs don't appear on copy.

2The first signs don't appear on the copy.

3This line does not appear on copy.

4No Akkadian translations for the lines 13-15.

1 The text has "his " instead of "her".

2 "Pure " is not in the Akkadian line.
